personal.about: Highly proficient Game Developer with over a decade of professional experience, specializing in Unity, multiplayer, and cross-platform game development, including VR/XR technologies. Expert in Unity DOTS, advanced game architecture design, and complex problem-solving.

projects.name: HighStreet Clamity VR
features: Class-based combat system with unique abilities and playstyles
Procedurally generated arenas with dynamic hazards
Challenging monsters and bosses with distinct behaviors
Solo and co-op multiplayer modes with matchmaking
Progression system with gear upgrades and skill trees
Social hub for players to connect and show off achievements
projects.shortDescription: Highstreet: Calamity – VR Roguelike Arena Combat in a Manic Metaverse. Protect the FOMO DUCKS! Highstreet: Calamity is a fast-paced, class-based VR roguelike brawler set in Solera, a chaotic world where talking ducks run businesses, pelicans have strong opinions, and monster portals are “just an average Tuesday.”
projects.description: Highstreet: Calamity is a fast-paced, class-based VR roguelike brawler set in Solera, a chaotic world where talking ducks run businesses, pelicans have strong opinions, and monster portals are “just an average Tuesday.” Players choose from unique classes with distinct abilities to battle waves of unpredictable enemies and bosses. Progression includes gear upgrades and branching skill paths. The game supports solo or co-op multiplayer with dynamic server allocation using GameLift. Social features include a lobby to connect with friends and enjoy the mayhem together.

contributions.title: DOTS Architecture Design
contributions.description: Led the design of a Unity DOTS-compatible architecture that cleanly separates logic between code and Unity scene component placement, enabling flexible and maintainable game development workflows across the team.

contributions.title: Full Project Architecture Leadership
contributions.description: Architected the entire project technical foundation including authentication flow, backend APIs, database schema, networking, server infrastructure, and all networked game core systems: spawn, health/mana/stats, combat, abilities, skills, consumables, monsters, monster AI, aggro system, AI coordinator, and interaction system — all synchronized over the network.

contributions.title: Matchmaking & Server Auto-Scalability
contributions.description: Led the design and implementation of the matchmaking server and server auto-scalability infrastructure, initially built on Unity Multiplay then migrated to Amazon GameLift after Unity divested the service.

contributions.title: Party & Friends System
contributions.description: Led the design and implementation of the party system and friends system, fully integrated into the matchmaking pipeline.

contributions.title: Data-Driven Game Design Pipeline
contributions.description: Designed and implemented a data-driven pipeline exposing game data via Excel sheets to game designers, allowing them to create and tweak abilities, stats, consumables, classes, and monster AI profiles without requiring code changes.

contributions.title: Custom DOTS State Machine with Editor Interface and network support
contributions.description: Designed and implemented a DOTS-based state machine with a full Unity Editor interface to support AI logic workflows. With no DOTS-native alternative available in Unity or the Asset Store at the time, this system became a foundational tool used extensively throughout the game's AI and general logic layers.

contributions.title: GameLift Fleet Management Tool [Vibe Coding]
contributions.description: Built a Python CLI tool to streamline GameLift server and fleet deployment and management, supporting saved fleet configurations to make deployments fast and repeatable.

contributions.title: Team DOTS Mentorship
contributions.description: Onboarded and mentored the entire team on Unity DOTS from zero prior knowledge. By the end of the project, team members could independently architect and implement their own DOTS systems — requiring only review rather than hands-on design involvement as at the project's start.

projects.name: Highstreet Campus
features: Metaverse entry point designed as a scalable social MMO hub
NFT shopping and social activity spaces across the campus
Travel pods connecting users from Campus to Highstreet Homes
VIP rooftop area with outdoor garden and social spaces
Salon shop and interactive interior spaces inside the campus
Dynamic lab system where players can swap lab interiors from an in-world console
Real-time synchronized interactions for shared systems like elevators and seats
Dynamic environment changes based on in-game events
projects.shortDescription: Highstreet Campus was built as the company's metaverse entry point: a large-scale WebGL social hub with MMO-oriented networking, NFT commerce, and connected world travel into Highstreet Homes.
projects.description: Highstreet Campus started as a social metaverse hub and strategic entry point for the company, with an early goal of building networking and infrastructure that could scale toward MMO requirements. The world combined social interaction spaces with NFT purchasing experiences, then expanded to include travel pods linking players to their homes in the previous Highstreet Homes project. The campus later introduced a VIP rooftop section with an outdoor garden, additional social activities, and a salon shop. One notable multiplayer challenge was a dynamic lab feature where players could switch lab interiors from an in-world console, requiring synchronized environment swaps while correctly handling other users already inside the affected area.

contributions.title: Custom MMO-Oriented Networking Layer
contributions.description: Implemented a custom network system on top of Mirror transport after evaluating available options and identifying limitations in Unity's default networking for MMO-scale goals. The architecture supported different object variants per context (server, local client, and remote client) instead of forcing a single prefab model, enabling much greater flexibility, including cross-platform representation needs. I also implemented chunk-based data sharing so the server synchronized state primarily between players in the same region.

contributions.title: Distributed Server Hierarchy Design
contributions.description: Designed and implemented a server hierarchy where the main server focused on authoritative synchronization, while specialized 'server-client' workers handled domain logic for entities such as elevators and AI-driven actors. The longer-term architecture targeted distributed authority so regional services could handle local combat/AI and write back required player-impact data, reducing core server load and enabling cost-efficient scaling by spinning regional services up only when needed.

contributions.title: Highstreet Homes Integration
contributions.description: Integrated the previous Highstreet Homes experience into Campus despite both projects originally using different multiplayer structures. Delivered stable cross-project travel flow through travel pods and unified player transitions between systems.

contributions.title: Unified Multi-Source Inventory System
contributions.description: Built a layered inventory system combining NFTs, backend/unlockable items, location-specific local items, and additional Web3-related internal APIs. Implemented adapters for each data source and a unified inventory abstraction on top, making future source additions straightforward. The system drove player clothing, access rules, and location eligibility across the experience.

contributions.title: Web Container Authentication Bridge
contributions.description: Implemented authentication and web-to-game communication using JavaScript bridge functions with callback-based JSON responses. Replaced fragile direct function-name coupling with a generic integration layer that isolated game internals from web implementation details and supported continuous profile/data updates from the website while players were connected.

contributions.title: Interaction Sync, UI, and Delivery Support
contributions.description: Owned synchronization of campus interactions (elevators, seats, player clothes, emotes, and other shared actions), and contributed across bug fixing, UI implementation, and multiple feature deliveries required to keep releases stable.

projects.name: Zinad Security Training Games
features: Multi-platform deployment (Web, VR, Kinect, Android, iOS)
Enterprise-grade security and data handling
Customizable content for different organizations
Engaging gameplay mechanics for learning retention
Accessible across different skill levels
Designed for large-scale employee training programs
projects.shortDescription: A suite of serious games for cybersecurity awareness training, delivered for enterprise clients across multiple platforms.
projects.description:
As Team Lead at Zinad, I led the development of a comprehensive suite of serious games for cybersecurity awareness training. The games translated complex security concepts into engaging, interactive learning experiences used by major enterprise clients.

Key projects included:
- Phishing Mania: An interactive game that teaches employees to identify phishing attempts
- Who Will Be Millionaire - Security Edition: A game-show format focused on cybersecurity knowledge
- The Office VR: An immersive VR experience for identifying clean desk policy violations
- Password Ninja: A fast-paced action game about password security
- Security Archery VR: A VR archery game for security question training

contributions.title: Game Team Lead
contributions.description: Led a team of three developers and an artist in the design and development of multiple cybersecurity training games, overseeing project timelines, coordinating tasks, and ensuring the delivery of high-quality training solutions for enterprise clients.

contributions.title: Collaboration with Cross-Functional Teams
contributions.description: Worked closely with web developers, DevOps teams, and QA teams to ensure smooth deployment and maintenance, while incorporating feedback for continuous improvement.

contributions.title: Refactored Legacy Games to Meet Enterprise Standards
contributions.description: Refactored legacy codebases of existing training games to improve performance, security, and maintainability. This included optimizing game logic, enhancing data handling practices, and ensuring compliance with enterprise security standards.

contributions.title: Port Games to New Platforms (WebGL, VR, Kinect, Android, iOS)
contributions.description: Led the porting of existing training games to new platforms, including WebGL for browser-based access, VR for immersive experiences, Kinect for motion-based interaction, and mobile platforms (Android and iOS) for on-the-go training. This involved adapting controls, optimizing performance, and ensuring a consistent user experience across all platforms.

contributions.title: Client Customization System
contributions.description: Implemented a flexible content management system that allowed for easy customization of game content to fit the specific security policies and branding requirements of different enterprise clients.

contributions.title: Project Template and Delivery Pipeline
contributions.description: Created a standardized project template and development pipeline for new training games, streamlining development and ensuring consistency across the suite. This reduced delivery time for new client training content from about 6 months to 2 months.

contributions.title: Web Bridge
contributions.description: Implemented authentication and web-to-game communication using JavaScript bridge functions with callback-based JSON responses. Replaced fragile direct function-name coupling with a generic integration layer that isolated game internals from web implementation details and supported continuous profile/data updates from the website while players were connected.

contributions.title: Designed and Implemented New Games
contributions.description: Designed and developed new training games based on emerging cybersecurity threats and client feedback, ensuring that the games remained relevant and effective in educating employees about current security risks.

contributions.title: ZiSoft Mobile App (iOS, Android)
contributions.description: Led development of the ZiSoft mobile app, which provided on-the-go access to training games and resources for employees. This included designing the app architecture, implementing core features, and adapting all games to run under one app while keeping each game in its own repository.

contributions.title: Interactive video player for training content
contributions.description: Developed an interactive video player for training content, allowing users to engage with the material through quizzes, annotations, and other interactive elements, enhancing the learning experience.

contributions.title: HTML Page View for Web Games
contributions.description: Implemented a system for displaying HTML pages inside web games to support phishing-email training, enabling realistic simulation of phishing emails and websites as part of training exercises.

projects.name: Highstreet Homes
features: Player homes with customizable furniture and decorations
NFT integration for inventory items and homes access
Cross-platform social interactions between web and VR users
Interactive activities within player homes
Travel pods connecting homes to the main campus
Player pets with interactive behaviors
projects.shortDescription: Highstreet Homes was designed as a social experience where players could purchase and customize their own homes with NFTs, interact with friends, and connect to the main campus through travel pods.
projects.description: Highstreet Homes was designed as a social experience where players could purchase and customize their own homes with NFTs, interact with friends, and connect to the main campus through travel pods. The project presented significant challenges in adapting Unity's native network system, which required extensive custom work to meet the experience's needs. The game supported cross-platform interactions between web and VR users, allowing them to socialize together regardless of their chosen platform. Players could decorate their homes with furniture and items purchased as NFTs, and also had interactive pets that added life to their virtual spaces.

contributions.title: Cross-Platform Multiplayer Architecture
contributions.description: adapted the Unity network system to support cross-platform multiplayer interactions between WebGL and VR users

contributions.title: Unified Multi-Source Inventory System
contributions.description: Built a layered inventory system combining NFTs, backend/unlockable items, location-specific local items, and additional Web3-related internal APIs. Implemented adapters for each data source and a unified inventory abstraction on top, making future source additions straightforward.

contributions.title: Furniture Placement System
contributions.description: Designed and implemented a furniture placement system that allowed players to customize their homes with items purchased as NFTs or provided by the location. The system included intuitive controls for placing, rotating, and moving furniture.

contributions.title: Sync Interactions
contributions.description: Implemented synchronization for player interactions within homes, such as Moving furniture , video player , emotes , change avatar ,sitting on furniture, interacting with pets, and using items. This included managing state across different platforms and ensuring a consistent experience for all players.

contributions.title: backend integration for home management
contributions.description: Implemented backend services to manage player homes, including saving and loading home configurations using a Firebase backend.

projects.name: Estimation Kings
features: Real-time multiplayer with global matchmaking
Comprehensive progression and leveling system
Social features including friend system and chat
Facebook integration for challenging friends
Multiple game environments and visual themes
Extensive avatar customization options
Quick communication with emotes and preset messages

Free-to-play with ethical monetization
projects.shortDescription: A polished multiplayer card game Published on Google Play. Features online matchmaking, progression system, social features, and cross-platform play.
projects.description:
Estimation Kings is a professionally Published multiplayer card game that brings the popular Middle Eastern trick-taking game to mobile platforms. Built with Phaser.js and featuring robust server architecture for real-time multiplayer gameplay.

The game successfully launched on Google Play and attracted a dedicated player base with its smooth gameplay, social features, and competitive progression system.

contributions.title: Flash-to-cocos2d-js Migration
contributions.description: Contributed to migrating the original Flash version to cocos2d-js for mobile deployment. Re-implemented core gameplay systems in JavaScript, optimized performance for mobile devices, and maintained feature parity while improving visuals and overall UX.

contributions.title: Stage 2: Unity Migration & SmartFox Server Refactor
contributions.description: Led the migration from cocos2d-js to Unity to improve cross-platform scalability and maintainability. Applied an MVC architecture to separate game logic from presentation, then refactored legacy SmartFoxServer code into a fully authoritative server model to improve stability, security, and gameplay consistency.

contributions.title: Phaser.js + Cordova Release Pipeline
contributions.description: Worked with the team lead on a new release built with Phaser.js and Cordova for web and mobile targets. Implemented the game navigation flow and built key front-end pages (server list, game settings, shop, and home screens) using HTML, CSS, and JavaScript.

projects.name: 3ashara Tawla
features: Two unique game modes: Ma7bosa and Thirteen
Advanced AI with multiple difficulty levels
Online multiplayer with matchmaking
Extensive character customization system
Multiple visual themes and boards
Social features and friend challenges
Tournament and ranked play modes
Cross-platform progression
projects.shortDescription: Modern take on the classic Middle Eastern backgammon game featuring online multiplayer, advanced AI, and extensive customization options.
projects.description:
3ashara Tawla is a comprehensive online multiplayer game based on Middle Eastern backgammon rules. The game features two distinct modes: 'Ma7bosa' and 'Thirteen', each offering unique gameplay experiences.

With sophisticated AI opponents, extensive character customization, and various game modes, 3ashara Tawla aims to be the definitive digital version of this beloved traditional game.

contributions.title: Core Gameplay Systems
contributions.description: Implemented the core game logic for both Ma7bosa and Thirteen modes, including turn management, move validation, and win condition checking.

contributions.title: AI Opponent Development
contributions.description: Designed and implemented an advanced AI system with multiple difficulty levels, providing a challenging experience for players of all skill levels.

contributions.title: Online Multiplayer Implementation
contributions.description: Built the online multiplayer infrastructure, including matchmaking, real-time game state synchronization, and cross-platform progression support.

projects.name: ComfyUI-QualityOfLifeSuit
features: Custom nodes for enhanced workflow efficiency
Quality-of-life improvements for ComfyUI interface
Integration with stable diffusion pipelines
Active community with 176+ GitHub stars
Regular updates and maintenance
Open-source and well-documented
projects.shortDescription: A comprehensive suite of quality-of-life nodes for ComfyUI, the powerful stable diffusion GUI. Featured in the ComfyUI ecosystem with 176 stars and 23 forks on GitHub.
projects.description:
ComfyUI-QualityOfLifeSuit is a collection of custom nodes designed to enhance the ComfyUI experience for AI artists and developers. This toolkit provides essential utilities and workflow improvements for stable diffusion workflows.

With over 176 stars on GitHub, this project has become a valuable resource in the ComfyUI community, helping artists and developers streamline their creative workflows.

contributions.title: Ai prompt enhancer node
contributions.description: Implemented a node that uses AI to enhance and expand text prompts for stable diffusion, allowing users to generate more detailed and creative outputs with minimal effort.

contributions.title: Open AI APIs as Nodes (ChatGPT, DALL-E)
contributions.description: Integrated OpenAI APIs as nodes within ComfyUI, enabling advanced AI functionalities and seamless interaction with external AI services.

contributions.title: Equation Node
contributions.description: Created a versatile equation node that allows users to perform complex mathematical operations within their ComfyUI workflows, enhancing the flexibility and power of their AI pipelines.

contributions.title: Text2image node
contributions.description: Developed a custom Text2Image that convert text to image which can be used as mask or soemthing in the workflow

projects.name: Portfolio & JSON Visual Editor
features: Vibe coded portfolio website with a clean, responsive UI
Built-in visual editor for portfolio.json (no manual raw JSON editing required)
Schema-aware add/remove flows to protect data structure integrity
Section and item focus mode for fast content editing
Media preview support for images and screenshots during editing
projects.shortDescription: A vibe coded portfolio project built with React and TypeScript, featuring a full visual editor for managing portfolio content safely.
projects.description: This portfolio is a vibe coded project focused on both presentation quality and practical content management. Beyond the public-facing portfolio pages, it includes an integrated visual editor that lets content be updated through structured UI controls instead of manual JSON text editing. The editor is schema-aware, supports nested sections, and helps prevent structural mistakes while editing projects, experience, education, and media fields.

contributions.title: Vibe Coded UX and Frontend Architecture
contributions.description: Designed and implemented the portfolio UI and interaction model using React, TypeScript, and Tailwind CSS, with an emphasis on readability, speed, and modern visual style.

contributions.title: Visual JSON Editor
contributions.description: Built a schema-driven editor for portfolio.json with nested field editing, array item management, and focused editing workflows that reduce content update friction.

contributions.title: Data Safety and Media Editing Enhancements
contributions.description: Implemented structure-safe creation flows, section/item navigation behavior, and media editing improvements including preview-first UX for image-related fields.

projects.name: Unreal 5 C++ Developer: Code Your Own Unreal Games
features: Battle Blaster: Multiplayer shooter with advanced combat mechanics
ShooterSam: Third-person shooter with sophisticated AI
Dungeon Escape: Atmospheric puzzle-action game
projects.shortDescription: Personal projects built to learn Unreal Engine, featuring a multiplayer shooter, an AI-driven third-person shooter, and a puzzle-action game.
projects.description: These projects were developed as part of my self-driven learning journey with Unreal Engine. Each game focused on different aspects of Unreal's capabilities, from multiplayer networking and combat systems in Battle Blaster, to AI behavior and third-person mechanics in ShooterSam, and atmospheric design in Dungeon Escape. These projects allowed me to gain hands-on experience with Unreal Engine's C++ programming, Blueprints visual scripting, and overall game development workflows within the engine.

contributions.title: Dungeon Escape
contributions.description: Developed an atmospheric puzzle-action game that emphasizes environmental storytelling and player immersion through level design and visual effects.

contributions.title: Battle Blaster
contributions.description: Designed and implemented a multiplayer shooter with advanced combat mechanics, including cover systems, weapon variety, and dynamic environments.

contributions.title: ShooterSam [in progress]
contributions.description: Created a third-person shooter featuring sophisticated AI opponents with varied behaviors and tactics, showcasing Unreal's AI capabilities.

projects.name: Rhythm Attack VR
features: Immersive VR gameplay with motion controls
Dynamic difficulty system that adapts to player skill
Original soundtrack and sound design
Custom 3D art and character designs
Hand-crafted visual effects
Rhythm-based combat mechanics
Award-winning ITI graduation project
projects.shortDescription: Award-winning VR rhythm game combining music, action, and innovative gameplay. ITI graduation project featuring custom art, music, and VFX - 100% original content.
projects.description:
Rhythm Attack is an innovative VR rhythm game that merges music, action, and strategy. Players must defeat rhythm monsters by slicing projectiles to the beat, then attacking when enemies are vulnerable. The game features escalating difficulty that adapts to player performance.

This project was our graduation project from the ITI Game Development program, showcasing our skills across all aspects of game development including programming, art, music composition, and visual effects - all created entirely by our team.

contributions.title: Lead Developer & Co-Designer
contributions.description: Led the development of core gameplay systems, including rhythm synchronization, combat mechanics, and dynamic difficulty. Collaborated on overall game design and flow.

contributions.title: Created Scriptable Object-Based Data Architecture
contributions.description: Designed and implemented a flexible data architecture using Unity's Scriptable Objects. which provided high felxibility for the team to create and tweak game content (enemies, levels, music cues, etc) without requiring code changes. This system became a foundational tool for the entire project and was used extensively across all content creation.

contributions.title: Audio Synchronization System
contributions.description: Implemented a robust audio synchronization system that ensured precise timing between music beats and gameplay events, creating a seamless rhythm-based experience.

contributions.title: Visual Effects Implementation
contributions.description: Designed and implemented custom visual effects that enhanced the game's immersive atmosphere and provided feedback for player actions.

projects.name: DOTS Behaviour Tree
features: ECS-native behaviour tree execution with nodes baked into entities
Visual node-graph editor for authoring trees in Unity
Runtime inspector to watch node status transitions live during play mode
Built-in composite nodes: Sequencer, Selector, Parallel, Random, and Repeater
Condition combinators: AND, OR, and NOT for flexible decision gating
Interrupt system for high-priority preemption and control handoff
Script templates for custom Action, Condition, Composite, and Interrupt nodes
projects.shortDescription: A high-performance Behaviour Tree framework for Unity DOTS/ECS with a visual editor, runtime inspector, interrupts, and extensible custom node templates.
projects.description:
Dots Behaviour Tree is a Behaviour Tree framework built for Unity DOTS (Data-Oriented Technology Stack) on top of ECS architecture. It focuses on high-performance AI decision-making by baking authoring nodes into ECS entities and executing them through ECS systems. The framework includes a visual editor for graph-based authoring, a runtime inspector for live debugging, rich composite nodes, condition combinators, and interrupt support for high-priority behaviour preemption. It also provides script templates to accelerate creation of custom Action, Condition, Composite, and Interrupt nodes.

Requirements from the README include Unity 6000.0.23f1+ with Entities 1.3.2+ (Burst compatible), and the demo scene runs with URP 17.0.4+.

contributions.title: ECS-Native Behaviour Tree Runtime
contributions.description: Implemented a DOTS-first execution model where authored tree nodes are baked into ECS entities and updated by ECS systems for scalable runtime performance.

contributions.title: Visual Authoring + Runtime Inspector Tooling
contributions.description: Built editor tooling for graph-based tree authoring and runtime state inspection, enabling faster iteration and easier debugging of node state transitions.

contributions.title: Extensible Node, Condition, and Interrupt Architecture
contributions.description: Designed an extensible framework with templates and base classes for custom Action/Condition/Interrupt nodes, including condition combinators and interrupt preemption flows.

projects.name: Scriptable Object Unity Tools
features: SO State Machine: Scriptable Object-based state machine system with visual debugging
SO Events & Variables: Event-driven architecture system for decoupled communication
SO System: Comprehensive Scriptable Object framework for data-driven design
projects.shortDescription: A suite of Unity tools leveraging Scriptable Objects to enable data-driven development, decoupled architecture, and visual debugging for game systems.
projects.description: This collection of Unity tools is designed to enhance game development workflows by leveraging the power of Scriptable Objects. The tools include a state machine system with visual debugging, an event-driven architecture for decoupled communication, and a comprehensive framework for data-driven design. These tools are open-source  and have been used in various projects to improve code organization, flexibility, and maintainability.

contributions.title: SO State Machine
contributions.description: Designed and implemented a Scriptable Object-based state machine system that allows developers to create complex state behaviors without writing code, featuring visual debugging tools for easy troubleshooting.

contributions.title: SO Events & Variables
contributions.description: Created an event-driven architecture system using Scriptable Objects that enables decoupled communication between game systems, allowing for more modular and maintainable code.

contributions.title: SO System
contributions.description: Developed a comprehensive framework for data-driven design in Unity using Scriptable Objects, providing a structured approach to managing game data and logic without hardcoding dependencies.

projects.name: Optimized Unity Scroll List
features: Virtual scrolling with object pooling so only visible items are instantiated
Smooth handling of 1000+ items with low draw calls and mobile-friendly performance
Supports both horizontal and vertical ScrollRect layouts
Responsive rebuilding when the viewport size changes
OnItemShow and OnItemHide callbacks for clean data binding on recycled items
Includes a decoupled ScriptableObject-based demo architecture
projects.shortDescription: High-performance Unity scroll list system with pooled, virtualized items for large datasets and smooth scrolling.
projects.description: Optimized Unity Scroll List is a Unity UI tooling project focused on rendering very large ScrollRect datasets efficiently by recycling a fixed pool of item views instead of instantiating one object per entry. The repo includes a working inventory demo, lifecycle callbacks for rebinding pooled items, support for horizontal and vertical lists, and a lightweight ScriptableObject-driven architecture for decoupled UI communication.

contributions.title: Virtualized ScrollRect Pooling System
contributions.description: Built the core ListOptmizer component to calculate visible ranges, recycle pooled UI items, and maintain front and rear filler elements so large lists scroll smoothly without the cost of thousands of instantiated objects.

contributions.title: Inspector-Friendly Item Lifecycle Hooks
contributions.description: Implemented OnItemShow and OnItemHide callback flows, plus OptmizedListItem UnityEvent hooks, so recycled cells can be rebound cleanly from code or directly through the Unity Inspector.

contributions.title: ScriptableObject Demo Integration
contributions.description: Structured the demo around ScriptableObject variables and event channels to keep inventory data, selection state, and UI updates decoupled while still being easy to wire up in-editor.
